title: dianthus seguieri subsp. cadevallii
intro: Dianthus seguieri subsp. cadevallii, also known as Cade or Juniper of Cadiz, is a plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as acne, eczema, and dermatitis due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dianthus seguieri subsp. cadevallii include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of essential oil, salves, and infusions, and can also be found in some traditional herbal medicines and skincare products.
